134 messages across 19 sessions (24 total) | 2026-01-16 to 2026-02-10

134
Messages
19
Sessions

Working Workflows

Test-Driven Development
Writing tests first leads to better code design and faster debugging cycles.

Friction Points

Context Loss Between Sessions
Repeatedly re-explaining project context slows down development work.
  • Forgetting previous architectural decisions
  • Re-explaining project conventions each session

Recommended Features

Persistent Memory
Would eliminate context re-explanation overhead across sessions.
Session-persistent memory for project context and decisions.

Usage Patterns

Iterative Refinement
Start with a rough draft, refine incrementally across multiple prompts.
Progressive improvement over single-pass generation.

Future Workflows

Automated Code Review
AI-powered review triggered automatically on every commit.
Start with a simple pre-commit hook that calls Claude for review.

Usage Narrative

Primarily used Claude Code for Python development and documentation tasks.

Most effective for rapid prototyping and iterative refinement workflows.
